summary: User proposed Deja Vue as a existing memory stack MCP project-local memory layer for coding agents.
Defined Deja Vue as repo-native memory that writes structured traces into `.memory-service/`.
Proposed `.memory-service/` directory with deja-vue.md, timeline.jsonl, current_state.md, decisions.md, handoff.md, agent_sessions, and diffs.
User clarified that Deja Vue captures relevant git changes.
Established that Deja Vue sits on top of git and records cognitive history, while git records mechanical history.
Outlined CLI/MCP commands: init, start, changed, decision, handoff, context.
User requested a Deja Vue capture for the current conversation.
Generated first Deja Vue memory pack from conversation context.
